text.NRSV: For if the eagerness is there, the gift is acceptable according to what one has—not according to what one does not have.
text.Rotherham: For, if the forwardness is set forth, according to what one may have, he is well approved, not according to what one hath not.
text.KJV: For if there bee first a willing minde, it is accepted according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not.
text.NABRE: For if the eagerness is there, it is acceptable according to what one has, not according to what one does not have;

text.NRSV: I do not mean that there should be relief for others and pressure on you, but it is a question of a fair balance between
text.Rotherham: For, not that unto others should be relief, and unto you distress [do I speak], but, by equality, in the present season, your surplus for their deficiency,—
text.KJV: For I meane not that other men bee eased, and you burthened:
text.NABRE: not that others should have relief while you are burdened, but that as a matter of equality

text.NRSV: your present abundance and their need, so that their abundance may be for your need, in order that there may be a fair balance.
text.Rotherham: In order that their surplus may come to be for your deficiency: that there may come about an equality:—
text.KJV: But by an equalitie: that now at this time your abundance may be a supply for their want, that their abundance also may be a supply for your want, that there may be equalitie,
text.NABRE: your surplus at the present time should supply their needs, so that their surplus may also supply your needs, that there may be equality.

text.NRSV: As it is written, “The one who had much did not have too much, and the one who had little did not have too little.”
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—He that [gathered] the much, had not more than enough, and, he that [gathered] the little, had not less,
text.KJV: As it is written, Hee that had gathered much, had nothing ouer, and hee that had gathered little, had no lacke.
text.NABRE: As it is written: “Whoever had much did not have more, and whoever had little did not have less.” Titus and His Collaborators.

text.NRSV: But thanks be to God who put in the heart of Titus the same eagerness for you that I myself have.
text.Rotherham: Thanks, however, unto God!—who is putting the same earnestness in your behalf in the heart of Titus,
text.KJV: But thankes bee to God which put the same earnest care into the heart of Titus for you.
text.NABRE: But thanks be to God who put the same concern for you into the heart of Titus,

text.NRSV: For he not only accepted our appeal, but since he is more eager than ever, he is going to you of his own accord.
text.Rotherham: In that, though, indeed, the exhortation, he welcomed; yet already being, greatly in earnest, of his own accord, hath he gone forth unto you.
text.KJV: For indeed he accepted the exhortation, but being more forward, of his owne accord he went vnto you.
text.NABRE: for he not only welcomed our appeal but, since he is very concerned, he has gone to you of his own accord.

text.NRSV: With him we are sending the brother who is famous among all the churches for his proclaiming the good news;
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, we also set forward, with him, the brother, whose praise in the Glad Tidings, [hath gone] through all the assemblies:—
text.KJV: And wee haue sent with him the brother, whose praise is in the Gospel, throughout all the Churches.
text.NABRE: With him we have sent the brother who is praised in all the churches for his preaching of the gospel.

text.NRSV: and not only that, but he has also been appointed by the churches to travel with us while we are administering this generous undertaking for the glory of the Lord himself and to show our goodwill.
text.Rotherham: Not only so, however, but he hath also been appointed by the assemblies, as a fellow-traveler with us in this favour, which is being ministered by us with a view to the Lord’s glory and our earnest desire:—
text.KJV: And not that onely, but who was also chosen of the Churches to trauaile with vs with this grace which is administred by vs to the glorie of the same Lord, and declaration of your readie minde.
text.NABRE: And not only that, but he has also been appointed our traveling companion by the churches in this gracious work administered by us for the glory of the Lord [himself] and for the expression of our eagerness.

text.NRSV: We intend that no one should blame us about this generous gift that we are administering,
text.Rotherham: Arranging this—lest anyone, upon us, should cast blame, in this munificence which is being ministered by us;
text.KJV: Auoyding this, that no man should blame vs in this aboundance which is administred by vs.
text.NABRE: This we desire to avoid, that anyone blame us about this lavish gift administered by us,

text.NRSV: for we intend to do what is right not only in the Lord’s sight but also in the sight of others.
text.Rotherham: For we provide things honourable, not only before [the] Lord, but also before men.
text.KJV: Prouiding for honest things, not onely in the sight of the Lord, but in the sight of men.
text.NABRE: for we are concerned for what is honorable not only in the sight of the Lord but also in the sight of others.

text.NRSV: And with them we are sending our brother whom we have often tested and found eager in many matters, but who is now more eager than ever because of his great confidence in you.
text.Rotherham: Moreover we have set forward, with them, our brother whom we have proved, in many things, ofttimes, earnest,—but, now, much more earnest, by reason of the great confidence [which he hath] towards you.
text.KJV: And we haue sent with them our brother, whom wee haue often times proued diligent in many things, but now much more diligent, vpon the great confidence which I haue in you.
text.NABRE: And with them we have sent our brother whom we often tested in many ways and found earnest, but who is now much more earnest because of his great confidence in you.

text.NRSV: As for Titus, he is my partner and co-worker in your service; as for our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, the glory of Christ.
text.Rotherham: Whether as regardeth Titus, he is a partner of mine, and, towards you, a fellow-worker. or our brethren, apostles of assemblies, and Christ’s glory.
text.KJV: Whether any doe enquire of Titus; he is my partner and fellow helper concerning you: or our brethren bee enquired of, they are the messengers of the Churches, and the glorie of Christ.
text.NABRE: As for Titus, he is my partner and co-worker for you; as for our brothers, they are apostles of the churches, the glory of Christ.

text.NRSV: Therefore openly before the churches, show them the proof of your love and of our reason for boasting about you.
text.Rotherham: The proof of your love, therefore, and of our boasting in your behalf, shew ye, unto them, in the face of the assemblies.
text.KJV: Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the Churches, the proofe of your loue, & of our boasting on your behalfe.
text.NABRE: So give proof before the churches of your love and of our boasting about you to them.

text.NRSV: Now it is not necessary for me to write you about the ministry to the saints,
text.Rotherham: For, indeed, concerning the ministry which is for the saints, it is, superfluous for me, to be writing to you;
text.KJV: For as touching the ministring to the Saints, it is superfluous for mee to write to you.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 God’s Indescribable Gift. Now about the service to the holy ones, it is superfluous for me to write to you,

text.NRSV: for I know your eagerness, which is the subject of my boasting about you to the people of Macedonia, saying that Achaia has been ready since last year; and your zeal has stirred up most of them.
text.Rotherham: For I know your forwardness of mind—of which, in your behalf, I am boasting unto Macedonians, that, Achaia, hath been prepared for a year past,—and, your zeal, hath stirred up the greater number of them.
text.KJV: For I know the forwardnesse of your mind, for which I boast of you to them of Macedonia, that Achaia was ready a yeere agoe, and your zeale hath prouoked very many.
text.NABRE: for I know your eagerness, about which I boast of you to the Macedonians, that Achaia has been ready since last year; and your zeal has stirred up most of them.

text.NRSV: But I am sending the brothers in order that our boasting about you may not prove to have been empty in this case, so that you may be ready, as I said you would be;
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless I have sent the brethren, that, our boast in your behalf, be not made void, in this respect, that, even as I was saying, ye may be prepared,—
text.KJV: Yet haue I sent the brethren, least our boasting of you should bee in vaine in this behalfe, that as I saide, yee may be readie.
text.NABRE: Nonetheless, I sent the brothers so that our boast about you might not prove empty in this case, so that you might be ready, as I said,

text.NRSV: otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find that you are not ready, we would be humiliated—to say nothing of you—in this undertaking.
text.Rotherham: Lest by any means, if there should come with me Macedonians, and find you unprepared, we,—not to say, ye,—should be put to shame in this confidence.
text.KJV: Lest happily if they of Macedonia come with mee, & find you vnprepared, wee (that wee say not, you) should bee ashamed in this same confident boasting.
text.NABRE: for fear that if any Macedonians come with me and find you not ready we might be put to shame (to say nothing of you) in this conviction.

text.NRSV: So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you, and arrange in advance for this bountiful gift that you have promised, so that it may be ready as a voluntary gift and not as an extortion.
text.Rotherham: Necessary, therefore, I regarded it, to exhort the brethren that they would go forward unto you, and make up beforehand your previously- promised blessing. The same, to be ready—thus, as a blessing, and not as of constraint.
text.KJV: Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren, that they would go before vnto you, and make vp before hand your bountie, whereof yee had notice before, that the same might bee readie, as a matter of bountie, not of couetousnesse.
text.NABRE: So I thought it necessary to encourage the brothers to go on ahead to you and arrange in advance for your promised gift, so that in this way it might be ready as a bountiful gift and not as an exaction.

text.NRSV: The point is this: the one who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the one who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
text.Rotherham: As to this, however, he that soweth sparingly, sparingly, also shall reap, and, he that soweth with blessings, with blessings, also shall reap:
text.KJV: But this I say, Hee which soweth sparingly, shall reape sparingly: and he which soweth bountifully, shall reape bountifully.
text.NABRE: Consider this: whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.

text.NRSV: Each of you must give as you have made up your mind, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
text.Rotherham: Each one, according as he hath purposed in his heart, not sorrowfully, nor of necessity,—for, a cheerful giver, God, loveth.
text.KJV: Euerie man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him giue; not grudgingly, or of necessitie: for God loueth a cheerefull giuer.
text.NABRE: Each must do as already determined, without sadness or compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.

text.NRSV: And God is able to provide you with every blessing in abundance, so that by always having enough of everything, you may share abundantly in every good work.
text.Rotherham: Moreover God is able to cause, every gracious gift, to superabound unto you, in order that, having in every thing, at every time, every kind of sufficiency of your own, ye may be superabounding unto every good work;
text.KJV: And God is able to make all grace abound towards you, that ye alwayes hauing all sufficiencie in all things, may abound to euery good worke,
text.NABRE: Moreover, God is able to make every grace abundant for you, so that in all things, always having all you need, you may have an abundance for every good work.

text.NRSV: As it is written, “He scatters abroad, he gives to the poor; his righteousness endures forever.”
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—He hath scattered abroad, he hath given to the needy,—his righteousness, abideth to futurity.
text.KJV: (As it is written: Hee hath dispersed abroad: Hee hath giuen to the poore: his righteousnesse remaineth for euer.
text.NABRE: As it is written: “He scatters abroad, he gives to the poor; his righteousness endures forever.”

text.NRSV: He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness.
text.Rotherham: Now, he that supplieth seed to the sower, and bread for eating, will supply and multiply your seed for sowing, and cause to grow your fruits of righteousness:
text.KJV: Now he that ministreth seede to the sower, both minister bread for your foode, and multiply your seede sowen, and encrease the fruites of your righteousnesse)
text.NABRE: The one who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed and increase the harvest of your righteousness.

text.NRSV: You will be enriched in every way for your great generosity, which will produce thanksgiving to God through us;
text.Rotherham: In every thing, being enriched, unto every kind of liberality,—which, indeed, worketh out, through us, thanksgiving unto God;
text.KJV: Being enriched in euery thing to al bountifulnes, which causeth through vs thankesgiuing to God.
text.NABRE: You are being enriched in every way for all generosity, which through us produces thanksgiving to God,

text.NRSV: for the rendering of this ministry not only supplies the needs of the saints but also overflows with many thanksgivings to God.
text.Rotherham: Because, the ministry of this public service, is not only towards filling up the deficiencies of the saints, but also superabounding, through the thanksgiving of many, unto God,—
text.KJV: For the administration of this seruice, not onely supplieth the want of the Saints, but is abundant also by many thanksgiuings vnto God,
text.NABRE: for the administration of this public service is not only supplying the needs of the holy ones but is also overflowing in many acts of thanksgiving to God.

text.NRSV: Through the testing of this ministry you glorify God by your obedience to the confession of the gospel of Christ and by the generosity of your sharing with them and with all others,
text.Rotherham: [Of many] who, through the proof of this ministry, are glorifying God for your acknowledged submission unto the glad-message of the Christ, and for the liberality of the fellowship, unto them, and unto all,—
text.KJV: Whiles by the experiment of this ministration, they glorifie God for your professed subiection vnto the Gospel of Christ, and for your liberall distribution vnto them, and vnto all men:
text.NABRE: Through the evidence of this service, you are glorifying God for your obedient confession of the gospel of Christ and the generosity of your contribution to them and to all others,

text.NRSV: while they long for you and pray for you because of the surpassing grace of God that has been given you.
text.Rotherham: Themselves also, with supplication on your behalf, longing after you, by reason of the surpassing favour of God [resting] upon you:—
text.KJV: And by their prayer for you, which long after you for the exceeding grace of God in you.
text.NABRE: while in prayer on your behalf they long for you, because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.

text.NRSV: Thanks be to God for his indescribable gift!
text.Rotherham: Thanks be unto God, for his unspeakable bounty!
text.KJV: Thanks be vnto God for his vnspeakeable gift.
text.NABRE: Thanks be to God for his indescribable gift!

text.NRSV: I myself, Paul, appeal to you by the meekness and gentleness of Christ—I who am humble when face to face with you, but bold toward you when I am away!—
text.Rotherham: Moreover, I, Paul, myself, exhort you, through the meekness and considerateness of the Christ,—I who, to look upon, indeed, am lowly toward you, but, absent, am bold towards you;—
text.KJV: Now I Paul my selfe beseech you, by the meekenes and gentlenesse of Christ, who in presence am base among you, but being absent, am bold toward you:
text.NABRE: IV. Paul’s Defense of His Ministry Chapter 10 Accusation of Weakness. Now I myself, Paul, urge you through the gentleness and clemency of Christ, I who am humble when face to face with you, but brave toward you when absent,

text.NRSV: I ask that when I am present I need not show boldness by daring to oppose those who think we are acting according to human standards.
text.Rotherham: I entreat, however, that, when present, I may not be bold with the assurance wherewith I think to be daring against some who account of us as though, according to flesh, we were walking,—
text.KJV: But I beseech you, that I may not bee bold when I am present, with that confidence wherewith I thinke to be bold against some, which thinke of vs as if wee walked according to the flesh.
text.NABRE: I beg you that, when present, I may not have to be brave with that confidence with which I intend to act boldly against some who consider us as acting according to the flesh.

text.NRSV: Indeed, we live as human beings, but we do not wage war according to human standards;
text.Rotherham: For, though, in flesh, we walk, not, according to flesh, do we war,—
text.KJV: For though we walke in the flesh, we doe not warre after the flesh:
text.NABRE: For, although we are in the flesh, we do not battle according to the flesh,

text.NRSV: for the weapons of our warfare are not merely human, but they have divine power to destroy strongholds. We destroy arguments
text.Rotherham: For, the weapons of our warfare, are not fleshly, but mighty, by God, unto a pulling down of strongholds,—
text.KJV: (For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling downe of strong holds.)
text.NABRE: for the weapons of our battle are not of flesh but are enormously powerful, capable of destroying fortresses. We destroy arguments

text.NRSV: and every proud obstacle raised up against the knowledge of God, and we take every thought captive to obey Christ.
text.Rotherham: When we pull down, calculations, and every height that uplifteth itself against the knowledge of God, and when we bring into captivity every thought unto the obedience of the Christ,
text.KJV: Casting down imaginations, and euery high thing that exalteth it selfe against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captiuitie euery thought to the obedience of Christ:
text.NABRE: and every pretension raising itself against the knowledge of God, and take every thought captive in obedience to Christ,

text.NRSV: We are ready to punish every disobedience when your obedience is complete.
text.Rotherham: And when we hold ourselves, in readiness, to avenge all disobedience, as soon as your obedience shall be fulfilled!
text.KJV: And hauing in a readinesse to reuenge all disobedience, when your obedience is fulfilled.
text.NABRE: and we are ready to punish every disobedience, once your obedience is complete.

text.NRSV: Look at what is before your eyes. If you are confident that you belong to Christ, remind yourself of this, that just as you belong to Christ, so also do we.
text.Rotherham: The things that lie on the surface, ye are looking at:—if anyone hath come to trust in himself that he is, Christ’s, this, let him reckon, again, with himself—that, even as, he, is Christ’s, so, also are, we.
text.KJV: Doe ye looke on things after the outward appearance? if any man trust to himselfe, that he is Christs, let him of himselfe thinke this againe, that as he is Christs, euen so are we Christs.
text.NABRE: Look at what confronts you. Whoever is confident of belonging to Christ should consider that as he belongs to Christ, so do we.

text.NRSV: Now, even if I boast a little too much of our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up and not for tearing you down, I will not be ashamed of it.
text.Rotherham: Yea, if, somewhat more abundantly, I should boast concerning our authority—which the Lord hath given for building up and not for pulling you down, I shall not be put to shame,—
text.KJV: For though I should boast somewhat more of our authority (which the Lord hath giuen vs for edification, and not for your destruction) I should not be ashamed:
text.NABRE: And even if I should boast a little too much of our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up and not for tearing you down, I shall not be put to shame.

text.NRSV: I do not want to seem as though I am trying to frighten you with my letters.
text.Rotherham: That I may not seem as though I would be terrifying you through means of my letters;
text.KJV: That I may not seeme as if I would terrifie you by letters.
text.NABRE: May I not seem as one frightening you through letters.

text.NRSV: For they say, “His letters are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech contemptible.”
text.Rotherham: Because, The letters, it is true (saith one), are weighty and strong, but, the presence of the body, is weak, and, the discourse, contemptible;—
text.KJV: For his letters (say they) are waighty and powerfull, but his bodily presence is weake, and his speach contemptible.
text.NABRE: For someone will say, “His letters are severe and forceful, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech contemptible.”

text.NRSV: Let such people understand that what we say by letter when absent, we will also do when present.
text.Rotherham: This, let such a one reckon—that, what we are, in our word, through means of letters, being absent, such, also, being present, are we, in our deed.
text.KJV: Let such a one thinke this: that such as we are in word by letters, when we are absent, such will we be also in deede when we are present.
text.NABRE: Such a person must understand that what we are in word through letters when absent, that we also are in action when present.

text.NRSV: We do not dare to classify or compare ourselves with some of those who commend themselves. But when they measure themselves by one another, and compare themselves with one another, they do not show good sense.
text.Rotherham: For we dare not class or compare ourselves with some who do, themselves, commend; but, they, among themselves, measuring, themselves, and comparing themselves with themselves, are without discernment!
text.KJV: For we dare not make our selues of the number, or compare our selues with some that commend themselues: but they measuring themselues by themselues, and comparing themselues amongst themselues, are not wise.
text.NABRE: Not that we dare to class or compare ourselves with some of those who recommend themselves. But when they measure themselves by one another and compare themselves with one another, they are without understanding.

text.NRSV: We, however, will not boast beyond limits, but will keep within the field that God has assigned to us, to reach out even as far as you.
text.Rotherham: We, however, not as to the things without measure, will boast ourselves, but, according to the measure of the limit which God apportioned unto us, as a measure—to reach as far as even you!
text.KJV: But we will not boast of things without our measure, but according to the measure of the rule, which God hath distributed to vs, a measure to reach euen vnto you.
text.NABRE: But we will not boast beyond measure but will keep to the limits God has apportioned us, namely, to reach even to you.

text.NRSV: For we were not overstepping our limits when we reached you; we were the first to come all the way to you with the good news of Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, not as though we were not reaching unto you, are we over-stretching ourselves, for, as far as even you, were we beforehand in the glad-message of the Christ:
text.KJV: For we stretch not our selues beyond our measure as though wee reached not vnto you, for wee are come as farre as to you also, in preaching the Gospel of Christ.
text.NABRE: For we are not overreaching ourselves, as though we did not reach you; we indeed first came to you with the gospel of Christ.

text.NRSV: We do not boast beyond limits, that is, in the labors of others; but our hope is that, as your faith increases, our sphere of action among you may be greatly enlarged,
text.Rotherham: Not, as to the things without measure, boasting ourselves in other men’s toils, but having, hope—your faith, growing—among you, to be enlarged, according to our limit for something beyond,—
text.KJV: Not boasting of things without our measure, that is, of other mens labours, but hauing hope, when your faith is increased, that wee shall bee enlarged by you, according to our rule abundantly.
text.NABRE: We are not boasting beyond measure, in other people’s labors; yet our hope is that, as your faith increases, our influence among you may be greatly enlarged, within our proper limits,

text.NRSV: so that we may proclaim the good news in lands beyond you, without boasting of work already done in someone else’s sphere of action.
text.Rotherham: Unto the regions beyond you, to carry the glad-message: not, within another man’s limit, as to the things made ready, to boast ourselves.
text.KJV: To preach the Gospel in the regions beyond you, and not to boast in another mans line of things made ready to our hand.
text.NABRE: so that we may preach the gospel even beyond you, not boasting of work already done in another’s sphere.

text.NRSV: “Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: He that boasteth, however, in the Lord, let him boast;
text.KJV: But he that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.
text.NABRE: “Whoever boasts, should boast in the Lord.”

text.NRSV: For it is not those who commend themselves who are approved, but those whom the Lord commends.
text.Rotherham: For, not he that commendeth himself, he, is approved, but he whom, the Lord, doth commend.
text.KJV: For, not he that commendeth himselfe is approued, but whom the Lord commendeth.
text.NABRE: For it is not the one who recommends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord recommends.

text.NRSV: I wish you would bear with me in a little foolishness. Do bear with me!
text.Rotherham: I could wish ye would bear with me as to some little foolishness,—Nay! do even bear with me!
text.KJV: Would to God you could beare with mee a little in my folly, & in deede beare with me.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 Preaching Without Charge. If only you would put up with a little foolishness from me! Please put up with me.

text.NRSV: I feel a divine jealousy for you, for I promised you in marriage to one husband, to present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
text.Rotherham: For I am jealous over you with a jealousy, of God; for I myself betrothed you unto one husband, to present, a chaste virgin, unto the Christ,—
text.KJV: For I am iealous ouer you with godly iealousie, for I haue espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
text.NABRE: For I am jealous of you with the jealousy of God, since I betrothed you to one husband to present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.

text.NRSV: But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by its cunning, your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
text.Rotherham: But I fear lest, by any means, as, the serpent, completely deceived Eve, in his craftiness, your minds should be corrupted from the singleness [and the chasteness] which are [due] unto the Christ.
text.KJV: But I feare lest by any meanes, as the Serpent beguiled Eue through his subtilty, so your mindes should bee corrupted from the simplicitie that is in Christ.
text.NABRE: But I am afraid that, as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning, your thoughts may be corrupted from a sincere [and pure] commitment to Christ.

text.NRSV: For if someone comes and proclaims another Jesus than the one we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or a different gospel from the one you accepted, you submit to it readily enough.
text.Rotherham: For, if, indeed, he that cometh, is proclaiming, another Jesus, whom we have not proclaimed, or, a different Spirit, ye are receiving, which ye had not received, or a different glad-message, which ye have never welcomed, ye are, well, bearing.
text.KJV: For if he that commeth preacheth another Iesus whome wee haue not preached, or if yee receiue another spirit, which ye haue not receiued, or another Gospel, which ye haue not accepted, yee might well beare with him.
text.NABRE: For if someone comes and preaches another Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received or a different gospel from the one you accepted, you put up with it well enough.

text.NRSV: I think that I am not in the least inferior to these super-apostles.
text.Rotherham: For I reckon not to have come a whit behind the exceeding overmuch apostles;
text.KJV: For, I suppose, I was not a whit behinde the very chiefest Apostles.
text.NABRE: For I think that I am not in any way inferior to these “superapostles.”

text.NRSV: I may be untrained in speech, but not in knowledge; certainly in every way and in all things we have made this evident to you.
text.Rotherham: And, even if uncultured in my discourse, certainly not in my knowledge,—but, in every way, having made [it] manifest, in all things, unto you.
text.KJV: But though I be rude in speach, yet not in knowledge; but we haue bene throughly made manifest among you in all things.
text.NABRE: Even if I am untrained in speaking, I am not so in knowledge; in every way we have made this plain to you in all things.

text.NRSV: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I proclaimed God’s good news to you free of charge?
text.Rotherham: Or, a sin, did I commit—abasing, myself, that, ye, might be exalted,—in that, free of charge, God’s glad-message, I announced unto you?
text.KJV: Haue I committed an offence in abasing my selfe, that you might be exalted, because I haue preached to you the Gospel of God freely?
text.NABRE: Did I make a mistake when I humbled myself so that you might be exalted, because I preached the gospel of God to you without charge?

text.NRSV: I robbed other churches by accepting support from them in order to serve you.
text.Rotherham: Other assemblies, I despoiled, receiving supplies that I might minister, unto you;
text.KJV: I robbed other Churches, taking wages of them to doe you seruice.
text.NABRE: I plundered other churches by accepting from them in order to minister to you.

text.NRSV: And when I was with you and was in need, I did not burden anyone, for my needs were supplied by the friends who came from Macedonia. So I kept myself from being a burden to you in any way, and will continue to do so.
text.Rotherham: And, being present with you, and having come short, I was not burdensome to anyone,—for, my deficiency, the brethren, coming from Macedonia, helped to make up;—and, in everything, without burden unto you, I kept, myself—and will keep!
text.KJV: And when I was present with you, and wanted, I was chargeable to no man: For that which was lacking to mee, the brethren which came from Macedonia supplied, and in all things I haue kept my selfe from being burthensome to you, and so will I keepe my selfe.
text.NABRE: And when I was with you and in need, I did not burden anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia supplied my needs. So I refrained and will refrain from burdening you in any way.

text.NRSV: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boast of mine will not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.
text.Rotherham: Truth of Christ is in me, that, this boasting, shall not be silenced unto me, in the regions of Achaia.
text.KJV: As the trueth of Christ is in mee, no man shall stop mee of this boasting in the regions of Achaia.
text.NABRE: By the truth of Christ in me, this boast of mine shall not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.

text.NRSV: And why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
text.Rotherham: Wherefore? Because I love you not? God, knoweth!
text.KJV: Wherefore? because I loue you not? God knoweth.
text.NABRE: And why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!

text.NRSV: And what I do I will also continue to do, in order to deny an opportunity to those who want an opportunity to be recognized as our equals in what they boast about.
text.Rotherham: But, what I am doing, I also will do,—that I may cut off the occasion of them who are wishing an occasion, that, wherein they are boasting themselves, they may be found even as also, we.
text.KJV: But what I doe, that I wil doe, that I may cut off occasion from them which desire occasion, that wherein they glory, they may bee found euen as we.
text.NABRE: And what I do I will continue to do, in order to end this pretext of those who seek a pretext for being regarded as we are in the mission of which they boast.

text.NRSV: For such boasters are false apostles, deceitful workers, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, such as these, are false apostles, deceitful workers, transfiguring themselves into apostles of Christ.
text.KJV: For such are false Apostles, deceitfull workers, transforming themselues into the Apostles of Christ.
text.NABRE: For such people are false apostles, deceitful workers, who masquerade as apostles of Christ.

text.NRSV: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
text.Rotherham: And no marvel! for, Satan himself, doth transfigure himself into a messenger of light!
text.KJV: And no marueile, for Sathan himselfe is transformed into an Angel of light.
text.NABRE: And no wonder, for even Satan masquerades as an angel of light.

text.NRSV: So it is not strange if his ministers also disguise themselves as ministers of righteousness. Their end will match their deeds.
text.Rotherham: No great thing, therefore, if, his ministers also, are transfiguring themselves as ministers of righteousness!—whose end, shall be according to their works.
text.KJV: Therefore it is no great thing if his ministers also bee transformed as the ministers of righteousnesse, whose end shall be according to their workes.
text.NABRE: So it is not strange that his ministers also masquerade as ministers of righteousness. Their end will correspond to their deeds.

text.NRSV: I repeat, let no one take me for a fool; but if you do, then accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.
text.Rotherham: Again, I say—let no one deem me to be, foolish; but, otherwise, at least, as foolish, give me welcome, that, I also, some little, may boast myself.
text.KJV: I say againe, Let no man thinke mee a foole; if otherwise, yet as a foole receiue me, that I may boast my selfe a little.
text.NABRE: Paul’s Boast: His Labors. I repeat, no one should consider me foolish; but if you do, accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.

text.NRSV: What I am saying in this self-confident boasting, I am saying not as the Lord would, but as a fool,
text.Rotherham: What I am saying, not according to the Lord, am I saying, but as in foolishness,—in this my boastful confidence!
text.KJV: That which I speake, I speake it not after the Lord, but as it were foolishly in this confidence of boasting.
text.NABRE: What I am saying I am not saying according to the Lord but as in foolishness, in this boastful state.

text.NRSV: since many boast according to human standards, I will also boast.
text.Rotherham: Since, many, are boasting after the flesh, I also, will boast;
text.KJV: Seeing that many glory after the flesh, I will glory also.
text.NABRE: Since many boast according to the flesh, I too will boast.

text.NRSV: For you gladly enjoy fools, being wise yourselves!
text.Rotherham: For, gladly, do ye bear with the foolish, being [yourselves], discreet,—
text.KJV: For ye suffer fooles gladly, seeing ye your selues are wise.
text.NABRE: For you gladly put up with fools, since you are wise yourselves.

text.NRSV: For you put up with it when someone makes slaves of you, or preys upon you, or takes advantage of you, or puts on airs, or gives you a slap in the face.
text.Rotherham: For ye bear with it—if anyone enslaveth you, if any devoureth, if anyone taketh, if anyone lifteth himself up, if anyone, on the face, doth smite you!
text.KJV: For ye suffer if a man bring you into bondage, if a man deuoure you, if a man take of you, if a man exalt himselfe, if a man smite you on the face.
text.NABRE: For you put up with it if someone enslaves you, or devours you, or gets the better of you, or puts on airs, or slaps you in the face.

text.NRSV: To my shame, I must say, we were too weak for that! But whatever anyone dares to boast of—I am speaking as a fool—I also dare to boast of that.
text.Rotherham: By way of disparagement, am I speaking,—as though, we, had been weak! whereas, in whatsoever anyone dareth, in foolishness I speak, I also, dare:—
text.KJV: I speake as concerning reproch, as though we had bene weake: howbeit, wherein soeuer any is bold, I speake foolishly, I am bold also.
text.NABRE: To my shame I say that we were too weak! But what anyone dares to boast of (I am speaking in foolishness) I also dare.

text.NRSV: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
text.Rotherham: Hebrews, are they? I also, Israelites, are they? I also, seed of Abraham, are they? I also,
text.KJV: Are they Hebrewes? so am I: are they Israelites? so am I: are they the seed of Abraham? so am I:
text.NABRE: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.

text.NRSV: Are they ministers of Christ? I am talking like a madman—I am a better one: with far greater labors, far more imprisonments, with countless floggings, and often near death.
text.Rotherham: Ministers of Christ, are they? As one deranged I am talking, I, above measure!—in toils, superabundantly, in prisons, superabundantly, in stripes, to excess, in deaths, oft,—
text.KJV: Are they ministers of Christ? I speake as a foole, I am more: in labors more abundant: in stripes aboue measure: in prisons more frequent: in deaths oft.
text.NABRE: Are they ministers of Christ? (I am talking like an insane person.) I am still more, with far greater labors, far more imprisonments, far worse beatings, and numerous brushes with death.

text.NRSV: Five times I have received from the Jews the forty lashes minus one.
text.Rotherham: From Jews, five times, forty-save-one, have I received,
text.KJV: Of the Iewes fiue times receiued I forty stripes saue one.
text.NABRE: Five times at the hands of the Jews I received forty lashes minus one.

text.NRSV: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I received a stoning. Three times I was shipwrecked; for a night and a day I was adrift at sea;
text.Rotherham: Thrice, have I been beaten with rods, once, have I been stoned, thrice, have I been shipwrecked, a day and night, in the deep, have I spent;
text.KJV: Thrice was I beaten with rods, once was I stoned: thrice I suffered shipwracke: a night and a day I haue bene in the deepe.
text.NABRE: Three times I was beaten with rods, once I was stoned, three times I was shipwrecked, I passed a night and a day on the deep;

text.NRSV: on frequent journeys, in danger from rivers, danger from bandits, danger from my own people, danger from Gentiles, danger in the city, danger in the wilderness, danger at sea, danger from false brothers and sisters;
text.Rotherham: In journeyings oft, in perils of rivers, in perils of robbers, in perils from my own race, in perils from Gentiles, in perils in the city, in perils in the desert, in perils by the sea, in perils among false brethren,
text.KJV: In iourneying often, in perils of waters, in perils of robbers, in perils by my owne countreymen, in perils by the heathen, in perils in the citie, in perils in the wildernesse, in perils in the sea, in perils among false brethren,
text.NABRE: on frequent journeys, in dangers from rivers, dangers from robbers, dangers from my own race, dangers from Gentiles, dangers in the city, dangers in the wilderness, dangers at sea, dangers among false brothers;

text.NRSV: in toil and hardship, through many a sleepless night, hungry and thirsty, often without food, cold and naked.
text.Rotherham: in toil and hardship, in watchings, oft, in hunger and thirst, in fasting, oft, in cold and nakedness:—
text.KJV: In wearinesse and painfulnesse, in watchings often, in hunger & thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakednes.
text.NABRE: in toil and hardship, through many sleepless nights, through hunger and thirst, through frequent fastings, through cold and exposure.

text.NRSV: And, besides other things, I am under daily pressure because of my anxiety for all the churches.
text.Rotherham: Apart from the things without, my daily care,—my anxiety for all the assemblies;—
text.KJV: Besides those things that are without, that which commeth vpon me dayly, the care of all the Churches.
text.NABRE: And apart from these things, there is the daily pressure upon me of my anxiety for all the churches.

text.NRSV: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to stumble, and I am not indignant?
text.Rotherham: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is caused to stumble, and, I, am not burning?
text.KJV: Who is weake, and I am not weake? who is offended, and I burne not?
text.NABRE: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is led to sin, and I am not indignant? Paul’s Boast: His Weakness.

text.NRSV: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.
text.Rotherham: If, to boast, is needful, in the things that concern my weakness, will I boast.
text.KJV: If I must needes glory, I will glory of the things which concerne mine infirmities.
text.NABRE: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.

text.NRSV: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus (blessed be he forever!) knows that I do not lie.
text.Rotherham: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus, knoweth—He who is blessed unto the ages—that I am not speaking falsely:
text.KJV: The God and Father of our Lord Iesus Christ, which is blessed for euermore, knoweth that I lie not.
text.NABRE: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus knows, he who is blessed forever, that I do not lie.

text.NRSV: In Damascus, the governor under King Aretas set a guard on the city of Damascus in order to seize me,
text.Rotherham: In Damascus, the governor under Aretas the king, was guarding the city of Damascenes, to apprehend me,—
text.KJV: In Damascus the gouernour vnder Aretas the King, kept the citie with a garison, desirous to apprehend mee.
text.NABRE: At Damascus, the governor under King Aretas guarded the city of Damascus, in order to seize me,

text.NRSV: but I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall, and escaped from his hands.
text.Rotherham: And, through a window, was I let down by the wall, and escaped his hands.
text.KJV: And through a window in a basket was I let downe, by the wall, and escaped his hands.
text.NABRE: but I was lowered in a basket through a window in the wall and escaped his hands.

text.NRSV: It is necessary to boast; nothing is to be gained by it, but I will go on to visions and revelations of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: To be boasting, is needful,—it is not, indeed, profitable, yet will I come to visions and revelations of [the] Lord:—
text.KJV: It is not expedient for me, doubtlesse, to glory, I wil come to visions and reuelations of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 I must boast; not that it is profitable, but I will go on to visions and revelations of the Lord.

text.NRSV: I know a person in Christ who fourteen years ago was caught up to the third heaven—whether in the body or out of the body I do not know; God knows.
text.Rotherham: I know a man in Christ, who, fourteen years ago, whether in the body, I know not, or out of the body, I know not, God, knoweth, such a one as this, caught away, as far as the third heaven;
text.KJV: I knewe a man in Christ aboue foureteene yeeres agoe, whether in the body, I cannot tell, or whether out of the body, I cannot tell, God knoweth: such a one, caught vp to the third heauen.
text.NABRE: I know someone in Christ who, fourteen years ago (whether in the body or out of the body I do not know, God knows), was caught up to the third heaven.

text.NRSV: And I know that such a person—whether in the body or out of the body I do not know; God knows—
text.Rotherham: And I know such a man as this, whether in the body or apart from the body, [I know not],—God, knoweth,
text.KJV: And I knew such a man (whether in the body, or out of the body, I cannot tell, God knoweth.)
text.NABRE: And I know that this person (whether in the body or out of the body I do not know, God knows)

text.NRSV: was caught up into Paradise and heard things that are not to be told, that no mortal is permitted to repeat.
text.Rotherham: How that he was caught away into paradise, and heard unspeakable things, which it is not allowable for a man to utter:
text.KJV: How that he was caught vp into Paradise, and heard vnspeakeable wordes, which it is not lawfull for a man to vtter.
text.NABRE: was caught up into Paradise and heard ineffable things, which no one may utter.

text.NRSV: On behalf of such a one I will boast, but on my own behalf I will not boast, except of my weaknesses.
text.Rotherham: On behalf of such a one as this, will I boast, but, on behalf of myself, will I not boast, save in my weaknesses;—
text.KJV: Of such a one will I glory, yet of my selfe I will not glory, but in mine infirmities.
text.NABRE: About this person I will boast, but about myself I will not boast, except about my weaknesses.

text.NRSV: But if I wish to boast, I will not be a fool, for I will be speaking the truth. But I refrain from it, so that no one may think better of me than what is seen in me or heard from me,
text.Rotherham: Although in fact, if I should wish to boast, I shall not be foolish, for, the truth, would I speak; but I forbear, lest anyone, unto me, should reckon above what he beholdeth me [to be], or heareth from me,—even by the exceeding greatness of the revelations.
text.KJV: For though I would desire to glory, I shall not be a foole: for I will say the trueth. But now I forbeare, lest any man should thinke of me aboue that which hee seeth me to bee, or that hee heareth of me:
text.NABRE: Although if I should wish to boast, I would not be foolish, for I would be telling the truth. But I refrain, so that no one may think more of me than what he sees in me or hears from me

text.NRSV: even considering the exceptional character of the revelations. Therefore, to keep me from being too elated, a thorn was given me in the flesh, a messenger of Satan to torment me, to keep me from being too elated.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, lest I should be unduly lifted up, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, a messenger of Satan, that he might buffet me,—lest I should be unduly lifted up.
text.KJV: And least I should bee exalted aboue measure through the abundance of the reuelations, there was giuen to me a thorne in the flesh, the messenger of Sathan to buffet me, lest I should be exalted aboue measure.
text.NABRE: because of the abundance of the revelations. Therefore, that I might not become too elated, a thorn in the flesh was given to me, an angel of Satan, to beat me, to keep me from being too elated.

text.NRSV: Three times I appealed to the Lord about this, that it would leave me,
text.Rotherham: In this behalf, thrice, besought I, the Lord, that it might depart from me;
text.KJV: For this thing I besought the Lord thrice, that it might depart from mee.
text.NABRE: Three times I begged the Lord about this, that it might leave me,

text.NRSV: but he said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for power is made perfect in weakness.” So, I will boast all the more gladly of my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may dwell in me.
text.Rotherham: And at once he said unto me—Sufficient for thee, is my favour, for, my power, in weakness, is made complete. Most gladly, therefore, will I rather boast in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may spread a tent over me.
text.KJV: And he said vnto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weaknes. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest vpon me.
text.NABRE: but he said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for power is made perfect in weakness.” I will rather boast most gladly of my weaknesses, in order that the power of Christ may dwell with me.

text.NRSV: Therefore I am content with weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions, and calamities for the sake of Christ; for when I am weak, then I am strong.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, I take pleasure in weaknesses, in insults, in necessities, in persecutions, and straits, in behalf of Christ; for, as soon as I am weak, then, am powerful.
text.KJV: Therefore I take pleasure in infirmities, in reproches, in necessities, in persecutions, in distresses for Christes sake: for when I am weake, then am I strong.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I am content with weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions, and constraints, for the sake of Christ; for when I am weak, then I am strong. Selfless Concern for the Church.

text.NRSV: I have been a fool! You forced me to it. Indeed you should have been the ones commending me, for I am not at all inferior to these super-apostles, even though I am nothing.
text.Rotherham: I have become foolish,—ye, compelled me:—I, in fact, ought, by you, to have been commended; for, not a whit, have I come behind the exceeding overmuch apostles,—even if I am nothing:
text.KJV: I am become a foole in glorying, ye haue compelled me. For I ought to haue beene commended of you: for in nothing am I behinde the very chiefest Apostles, though I be nothing.
text.NABRE: I have been foolish. You compelled me, for I ought to have been commended by you. For I am in no way inferior to these “superapostles,” even though I am nothing.

text.NRSV: The signs of a true apostle were performed among you with utmost patience—signs and wonders and mighty works.
text.Rotherham: The signs, indeed, of an apostle, were wrought out among you, in all endurance,—[both] in signs, and wonders, and mighty works:
text.KJV: Truely the signes of an Apostle were wrought among you in all patience, in signes and wonders, and mightie deeds.
text.NABRE: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with all endurance, signs and wonders, and mighty deeds.

text.NRSV: How have you been worse off than the other churches, except that I myself did not burden you? Forgive me this wrong!
text.Rotherham: For what is there wherein ye were made inferior to the rest of the assemblies,—save that, I myself, would not allow myself to be a burden unto you? Forgive me this wrong!
text.KJV: For what is it wherein yee were inferior to other Churches, except it bee that I my selfe was not burthensome to you? forgiue me this wrong.
text.NABRE: In what way were you less privileged than the rest of the churches, except that on my part I did not burden you? Forgive me this wrong!

text.NRSV: Here I am, ready to come to you this third time. And I will not be a burden, for I do not want your possessions but you; for children ought not to lay up for their parents, but parents for their children.
text.Rotherham: Lo! this third time, I am holding myself, in readiness, to come unto you, and I will not allow myself to be a burden; for I seek not yours, but you;—for, the children, ought not to lay up, for the parents, but the parents for the children;—
text.KJV: Behold, the third time I am readie to come to you, and I will not bee burthensome to you; for I seeke not yours, but you: for the children ought not to lay vp for the parents, but the parents for the children.
text.NABRE: Now I am ready to come to you this third time. And I will not be a burden, for I want not what is yours, but you. Children ought not to save for their parents, but parents for their children.

text.NRSV: I will most gladly spend and be spent for you. If I love you more, am I to be loved less?
text.Rotherham: And, I, most gladly, will spend, and be fully spent, for your souls:—If I, exceedingly, love you, am I, the less, loved?
text.KJV: And I wil very gladly spend and bee spent for you, though the more abundantly I loue you, the lesse I bee loued.
text.NABRE: I will most gladly spend and be utterly spent for your sakes. If I love you more, am I to be loved less?

text.NRSV: But granting that I myself did not burden you, I was crafty, you say, and got the better of you by guile.
text.Rotherham: But let it be!—I, myself, did not burden you,—Notwithstanding, being crafty, with guile, I caught you?
text.KJV: But be it so: I did not burthen you: neuerthelesse beeing craftie, I caught you with guile.
text.NABRE: But granted that I myself did not burden you, yet I was crafty and got the better of you by deceit.

text.NRSV: Did I take advantage of you through any of those whom I sent to you?
text.Rotherham: Was there, anyone of those whom I have sent unto you, through whom I have overreached you?
text.KJV: Did I make a gaine of you by any of them, whom I sent vnto you?
text.NABRE: Did I take advantage of you through any of those I sent to you?

text.NRSV: I urged Titus to go, and sent the brother with him. Titus did not take advantage of you, did he? Did we not conduct ourselves with the same spirit? Did we not take the same steps?
text.Rotherham: I exhorted Titus, and sent with him the brother,—Did, Titus, overreach you? Was it not, in the same spirit, we walked? Was it not, in the same steps?
text.KJV: I desired Titus, and with him I sent a brother: did Titus make a gaine of you? Walked wee not in the same spirit? walked wee not in the same steps?
text.NABRE: I urged Titus to go and sent the brother with him. Did Titus take advantage of you? Did we not walk in the same spirit? And in the same steps? Final Warnings and Appeals.

text.NRSV: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves before you? We are speaking in Christ before God. Everything we do, beloved, is for the sake of building you up.
text.Rotherham: All this time, think ye, that, unto you, we are making a defence? Before God in Christ, we speak;—but all these things, beloved, for your upbuilding.
text.KJV: Againe, thinke you that we excuse our selues vnto you? wee speake before God in Christ: but wee doe all things, dearely beloued, for your edifying.
text.NABRE: Have you been thinking all along that we are defending ourselves before you? In the sight of God we are speaking in Christ, and all for building you up, beloved.

text.NRSV: For I fear that when I come, I may find you not as I wish, and that you may find me not as you wish; I fear that there may perhaps be quarreling, jealousy, anger, selfishness, slander, gossip, conceit, and disorder.
text.Rotherham: For I fear—lest, by any means, when I come, not such as I wish, should I find you, and, I, should be found by you, such as ye do not wish;—lest, by any means, [I should find] strife, jealousy, outbursts of wrath, factions, railings, whisperings, puffed up pretensions, confusions;—
text.KJV: For I feare lest when I come, I shall not find you such as I would, and that I shall bee found vnto you such as ye would not, lest there bee debates, enuyings, wraths, strifes, backebitings, whisperings, swellings, tumults,
text.NABRE: For I fear that when I come I may find you not such as I wish, and that you may find me not as you wish; that there may be rivalry, jealousy, fury, selfishness, slander, gossip, conceit, and disorder.

text.NRSV: I fear that when I come again, my God may humble me before you, and I may have to mourn over many who previously sinned and have not repented of the impurity, sexual immorality, and licentiousness that they have practiced.
text.Rotherham: Lest, when I again come, my God should humble me in regard to you,—and I should grieve over many who had before sinned, and not repented of the impurity, and fornication, and wantonness which they had committed.
text.KJV: And least when I come againe, my God will humble mee among you, and that I shall bewaile many which haue sinned alreadie, and haue not repented of the vncleannesse, and fornication, and lasciuiousnesse which they haue committed.
text.NABRE: I fear that when I come again my God may humiliate me before you, and I may have to mourn over many of those who sinned earlier and have not repented of the impurity, immorality, and licentiousness they practiced.

text.KJV: This is the third time I am comming to you: in the mouth of two or three witnesses shal euery word be established.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 This third time I am coming to you. “On the testimony of two or three witnesses a fact shall be established.”
text.NRSV: This is the third time I am coming to you. “Any charge must be sustained by the evidence of two or three witnesses.”

text.KJV: I told you before, and foretell you as if I were present the second time, and being absent, now I write to them which heretofore haue sinned, and to all other, that if I come againe I will not spare:
text.NABRE: I warned those who sinned earlier and all the others, and I warn them now while absent, as I did when present on my second visit, that if I come again I will not be lenient,
text.NRSV: I warned those who sinned previously and all the others, and I warn them now while absent, as I did when present on my second visit, that if I come again, I will not be lenient—

text.KJV: Since ye seeke a proofe of Christ, speaking in me, which to you-ward is not weake, but is mightie in you.
text.NABRE: since you are looking for proof of Christ speaking in me. He is not weak toward you but powerful in you.
text.NRSV: since you desire proof that Christ is speaking in me. He is not weak in dealing with you, but is powerful in you.

text.KJV: For though hee was crucified through weaknesse, yet he liueth by the power of God: for wee also are weake in him, but wee shall liue with him by the power of God toward you.
text.NABRE: For indeed he was crucified out of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. So also we are weak in him, but toward you we shall live with him by the power of God.
text.NRSV: For he was crucified in weakness, but lives by the power of God. For we are weak in him, but in dealing with you we will live with him by the power of God.

text.KJV: Examine your selues, whether ye be in the faith: proue your owne selues. Know yee not your owne selues, how that Iesus Christ is in you, except ye be reprobates?
text.NABRE: Examine yourselves to see whether you are living in faith. Test yourselves. Do you not realize that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless, of course, you fail the test.
text.NRSV: Examine yourselves to see whether you are living in the faith. Test yourselves. Do you not realize that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless, indeed, you fail to meet the test!

text.KJV: But I trust that yee shall knowe that we are not reprobates.
text.NABRE: I hope you will discover that we have not failed.
text.NRSV: I hope you will find out that we have not failed.

text.KJV: Now I pray to God, that ye doe no euill, not that we should appeare approued, but that ye should doe þ which is honest, though we be as reprobates.
text.NABRE: But we pray to God that you may not do evil, not that we may appear to have passed the test but that you may do what is right, even though we may seem to have failed.
text.NRSV: But we pray to God that you may not do anything wrong—not that we may appear to have passed the test, but that you may do what is right, though we may seem to have failed.

text.KJV: For wee can doe nothing against the trueth, but for the trueth.
text.NABRE: For we cannot do anything against the truth, but only for the truth.
text.NRSV: For we cannot do anything against the truth, but only for the truth.

text.KJV: For wee are glad when wee are weake, and ye are strong: and this also we wish, euen your perfection.
text.NABRE: For we rejoice when we are weak but you are strong. What we pray for is your improvement.
text.NRSV: For we rejoice when we are weak and you are strong. This is what we pray for, that you may become perfect.

text.KJV: Therefore I write these things being absent, lest being present I should vse sharpnesse, according to the power which the Lord hath giuen me to edification, and not to destruction.
text.NABRE: I am writing this while I am away, so that when I come I may not have to be severe in virtue of the authority that the Lord has given me to build up and not to tear down.
text.NRSV: So I write these things while I am away from you, so that when I come, I may not have to be severe in using the authority that the Lord has given me for building up and not for tearing down.

text.KJV: Finally, brethren, farewell: Bee perfect, bee of good comfort, bee of one minde, liue in peace, and the God of loue and peace shalbe with you.
text.NABRE: V. Conclusion Finally, brothers, rejoice. Mend your ways, encourage one another, agree with one another, live in peace, and the God of love and peace will be with you.
text.NRSV: Finally, brothers and sisters, farewell. Put things in order, listen to my appeal, agree with one another, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.

text.KJV: Greet one another with an holy kisse.
text.NABRE: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the holy ones greet you.
text.NRSV: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the saints greet you.

text.KJV: All the Saints salute you.
text.NABRE: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ and the love of God and the fellowship of the holy Spirit be with all of you.
text.NRSV: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the communion of the Holy Spirit be with all of you.

text.KJV: The grace of the Lord Iesus Christ, and the loue of God, and the communion of the holy Ghost, be with you all. Amen. The second Epistle to the Corinthians, was written from Philippos a citie of Macedonia, by Titus and Lucas.

13.1: This third time, am I coming unto you:—at the mouth of two witnesses, or three, shall every matter be established.
13.2: I have said beforehand, and do say beforehand,—as present the second time, although now absent, unto them who before sinned, and unto all the rest,—that, if I come again, I will not spare.
13.3: Since, a test, ye are seeking, of the speaking in me of Christ;—who, unto you, is not weak, but is powerful in you,—
13.4: For, although he was crucified through weakness, yet is he living through God’s power,—for, although, we, are weak in him, yet shall we live with him, through God’s power [unto us]—
13.5: Be trying, yourselves, whether ye are in the faith, be putting, yourselves, to the test! Or do ye not recognise yourselves, seeing that, Jesus Christ, is in you,—unless perhaps ye fail in the testing.
13.6: I hope, however, that ye shall come to know, that, we, fail not in the testing!
13.7: But we pray unto God, that ye may do nothing base,—not that, we, may appear, accepted, but that, ye, the honourable thing, may be doing, even though, we, should be, as it were, rejected;
13.8: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth;
13.9: For we rejoice—whensoever, we, are weak, and, ye, are, strong. This, also we pray for—your restoration:—
13.10: For this cause, these things, while absent, am I writing, that I may not, when present, deal sharply—according to the authority which, the Lord, hath given me, for building up and not for pulling down.
13.11: Finally, brethren, farewell! Be getting restored, be taking encouragement, be of one mind, live in peace,—and, the God of love and peace, will be with you.
13.12: Salute ye one another with a holy kiss.
13.14: The favour of our Lord Jesus [Christ], and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit, be with you all.

The grace of the Lord Iesus Christ, and the loue of God, and the communion of the holy Ghost, be with you all. Amen. The second Epistle to the Corinthians, was written from Philippos a citie of Macedonia, by Titus and Lucas.
